perturbation.original_text: “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder', the definition of 'Evaluation Material' becomes vague. It introduces uncertainty because the receiving party can now claim that documents created are not covered by the CDA, despite being derived from disclosed Evaluation Material. This contradicts the initial intent of the agreement to protect disclosed information and subsequent analyses based on it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction and will use such Evaluation Materials only for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction. Each party agrees to use reasonable measures to protect against the loss, theft or unauthorized use of the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Evaluation Material' as including documents 'which contain or are based upon' the furnished material. The changed text removes this qualifier. The contradicted text states that each party will protect the other party's 'Evaluation Material'. By removing the phrase 'which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder', the changed text introduces a contradiction, because the receiving party can now claim that documents created are not covered by the CDA, despite being derived from disclosed Evaluation Material, thus failing to protect information as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting '(including all documents based thereon)', it weakens the obligation to return or destroy derivative works. This creates an inconsistency with Section 1, which defines Evaluation Material to include 'notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents'. If these derivative documents are not explicitly required to be returned or destroyed, it contradicts the broader aim of protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Evaluation Material. “Evaluation Material” also includes notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain or are based upon the Evaluation Material furnished to such party hereunder. The term “Evaluation Material” does not include information that (i) is or becomes a matter of public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known or reasonably should be known by the receiving party to be bound by a duty of confidentiality with respect to such information; (iii) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the Evaluation Material; or (v) is disclosed under operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase '(including all documents based thereon)' from the requirement to return or destroy 'Evaluation Material'. The contradicted text defines 'Evaluation Material' to include derivative documents such as 'notes, analyses, compilations, studies, plans, interpretations or other documents'. By removing the explicit mention of these derivative documents, the changed text creates a contradiction because it weakens the obligation to return or destroy them, conflicting with the definition and the aim of protecting confidential information as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that a party or its Representatives is required by Law, rule or stock exchange regulation to disclose any of the other party’s Evaluation Material or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, the party required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice of any such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order, other remedy or waiver is not obtained, the party required to make the disclosure shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material that such party determines, alter consulting with counsel, is required to disclose and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party’s Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that a party or its Representatives is required by Law, rule or stock exchange regulation to disclose any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the party required to make such disclosure shall, to the extent practicable, provide the other party with prior written notice of any such requirement so that the other party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order, other remedy or waiver is not obtained, the party required to make the disclosure shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material that such party determines, alter consulting with counsel, is required to disclose and shall exercise all reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the other party’s Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction' from Section 6, the agreement creates an inconsistency. It still requires notice and consultation if Evaluation Material must be disclosed by law, but no longer explicitly addresses the scenario where the *existence* of the transaction itself must be disclosed. This contradicts the general confidentiality aims of the agreement regarding the Transaction and Evaluation material related to it, as previously indicated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, neither it nor its Representatives will disclose to any other person any of the other party’s Evaluation Material, the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction, or any terms or other facts relating to the Transaction and will use such Evaluation Materials only for the purpose of evaluating a Transaction. Each party agrees to use reasonable measures to protect against the loss, theft or unauthorized use of the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'or the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction' from the original text, which creates a contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly states that neither party will disclose 'the fact that discussions between the parties are taking place concerning the Transaction' without prior written consent. By removing this phrase from the section regarding disclosure requirements when legally compelled, the agreement no longer addresses this specific scenario, thus contradicting the confidentiality obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'.
